content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:
- MongoDB 3-member replica set running in the `mongodb` namespace
- Prometheus running in `monitoring` namespace, with `--web.enable-lifecycle` (so we can reload via `/-/reload`)
- Config is loaded from the `prometheus-config` ConfigMap mounted at `/etc/prometheus/`

My plan:
1. Deploy a `mongodb-exporter` as a standalone deployment in the `mongodb` namespace. I'll use the `percona/mongodb_exporter` image or `bitnami/mongodb-exporter`.
2. Create a Service to expose the exporter on port 9216.
3. Update the Prometheus config to scrape this new target, then trigger a config reload.

I'll go with `percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40` since it's the standard image. The MongoDB connection string will point to all three replica set members using their headless service DNS names within the cluster.

Actually, I can simplify this by just connecting to a single pod like `mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017` since the headless service DNS will resolve appropriately. But first I need to check if there are authentication requirements for the MongoDB connection.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb mongo-rs-0 -- mongosh --quiet --eval "db.adminCommand({getCmdLineOpts:1})" 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'auth|security' || echo "no auth output"
input.description: Check if MongoDB has auth enabled

content.thinking: Good, no auth is enabled and the replica set is healthy. Now let me deploy the mongodb_exporter. I'll use the `percona/mongodb_exporter` image. Let me check what's available or if there's a pre-pulled image.

input.command: docker images 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'mongo.*export|percona|bitnami' || true; crictl images 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'mongo.*export|percona|bitnami' || true

content.thinking: No mongodb-exporter image pre-pulled. Let me check what images are in the kind cluster.

input.command: docker exec karma-1-control-plane crictl images 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'mongo|export|percona'
input.description: Check images in kind cluster node

content.thinking:
There is `percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40` already in the cluster. Let me deploy it.

The mongodb_exporter connection string for a replica set without auth would be:
`mongodb://mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017,mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017,mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017/?directConnection=true`

Actually, for a simple check, I'll just connect to any one of the pod IPs or use the headless service. 

For the exporter to work, I need to pass the MongoDB connection URI via the `MONGODB_URI` environment variable. I'll set up a Deployment for the exporter in the mongodb namespace, expose it with a Service on port 9216, add a scrape job to the Prometheus ConfigMap, and trigger a reload. Since there's no authentication configured, I can just point it at one of the replica set members.

content.text: The `percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40` image is already in the cluster. I'll deploy it as a standalone pod, expose it via a Service, then update the Prometheus scrape config.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-exporter
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mongodb-exporter
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: mongodb-exporter
        image: percona/mongodb_exporter:0.40
        args:
        - --mongodb.uri=mongodb://mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:27017
        - --web.listen-address=:9216
        - --collect-all
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9216
          name: metrics
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: mongodb-exporter
  namespace: mongodb
  labels:
    app: mongodb-exporter
spec:
  selector:
    app: mongodb-exporter
  ports:
  - name: metrics
    port: 9216
    targetPort: 9216
EOF

content.text: Now update the Prometheus ConfigMap to add the MongoDB scrape job:

input.command:
kubectl patch configmap prometheus-config -n monitoring --type=merge -p '{
  "data": {
    "prometheus.yml": "global:
  scrape_interval: 30s
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "prometheus"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["localhost:9090"]
  - job_name: "mongodb"
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["mongodb-exporter.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:9216"]
"
  }
}'
input.description: Patch Prometheus ConfigMap to add MongoDB scrape job

content.text: Wait for the exporter to be ready, then trigger a Prometheus config reload:

input.description: Wait for mongodb-exporter deployment to be ready

input.command:
# Verify the exporter is exposing metrics
kubectl exec -n mongodb curl-test -- curl -sf http://mongodb-exporter.mongodb.svc.cluster.local:9216/metrics | grep -E '^mongodb_up'

content.text: The exporter reports `mongodb_up 1`. Now trigger the Prometheus hot-reload:

input.command:
PROM_POD=$(kubectl get pod -n monitoring -l app=prometheus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}' 2>/dev/null || kubectl get pod -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
kubectl exec -n monitoring $PROM_POD -- wget -qO- --post-data='' http://localhost:9090/-/reload && echo "reload sent"

# Wait a moment then verify Prometheus is actually scraping MongoDB
sleep 35
PROM_POD=$(kubectl get pod -n monitoring -l app=prometheus -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}' 2>/dev/null || kubectl get pod -n monitoring -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
kubectl exec -n monitoring $PROM_POD -- wget -qO- 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=mongodb_up'
input.description: Query Prometheus for mongodb_up metric after reload

until kubectl exec -n monitoring $PROM_POD -- wget -qO- 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=mongodb_up' | grep -q '"value"'; do sleep 5; done
kubectl exec -n monitoring $PROM_POD -- wget -qO- 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=mongodb_up'
